fields.Name: Path LMS
fields.Description: Path LMS (Blue Sky eLearn) Reporting API connector — pull-only GraphQL analytics over 16 report query surfaces. Two-step bearer auth (applicationId + applicationSecret then 12-hour JWT); no writes. All data via POST to the single GraphQL endpoint.

Configuration.AuthFlowDescription: POST x-www-form-urlencoded {applicationId, applicationSecret} to /api/v1/getToken. Response: {"token": "Bearer <jwt>"}. Extract the JWT portion (strip 'Bearer ' prefix if present); set Authorization: Bearer <jwt> on all subsequent GraphQL requests. Token lifetime is 12 hours; no refresh token — re-mint on 401 or on expiry.
Configuration.RefreshNote: No refresh token issued. On token expiry or 401, re-exchange applicationId + applicationSecret for a new token.

PaginationDefaults.description: Integer offset + limit pagination. offset=0 default, limit max 150 per API docs. Increment offset by limit on each page; stop when returned count < limit.
PaginationDefaults.TotalCountFieldPatternNote: Account and Team types expose a <subReportName>TotalCount: Int! companion field for each paginated sub-report array (e.g. users -> usersTotalCount). Not all sub-fields have a TotalCount — where absent, stop-when-count-less-than-limit termination applies.

Configuration.IncrementalSyncNote: The SDL exposes startDate/endDate filter arguments on many queries but these filter by event date, not by record modification time. No updatedAt/modifiedSince watermark field exists in any type. Content-hash idempotency + StableOrderingKey (id) is the correct resume pattern.
Configuration.WebhooksNote: No webhook or subscription endpoints documented in the GraphQL SDL or any credential-free source. SDL has 0 subscriptions.
Configuration.BulkOperationsNote: No batch or bulk mutation endpoints. All data retrieval is paginated GraphQL query. No mutations exist in the SDL.
Configuration.APIVersioningStrategyNote: Token endpoint is /api/v1/getToken and CSV endpoint is /api/v1/csv. GraphQL endpoint is /graphql (no version suffix). The v1 prefix is present on REST utility endpoints only; the GraphQL schema itself is unversioned.
Configuration.TokenRefreshStrategyNote: No refresh token issued. Connector must cache (token + expiry = now + 12h). On expiry or 401, call /api/v1/getToken again with applicationId + applicationSecret from the credential store.

ErrorResponseShape.description: GraphQL errors are returned in the standard {errors: [{message, extensions}]} envelope. HTTP 500 with errors array indicates context/auth failure. HTTP 200 with errors array indicates a query-level error. HTTP 401 indicates token expiry or missing token.
ErrorResponseShape.authErrorPattern: {"errors":[{"message":"Context creation failed: JsonWebTokenError: jwt must be provided","extensions":{"code":"INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR"}}]}

WriteCapability.Note: The Path LMS Reporting API has 0 mutations and 0 subscriptions in the SDL. It is a read-only reporting surface. Write operations require the separate admin REST API at pathlms.com/api/v1 which is out of scope for this connector.

Configuration.ConcurrencyControlNote: Read-only API. No ETag, version, or If-Match headers documented. ConcurrencyControl=none because no mutations exist.
Configuration.DeleteSemanticsNote: Pull-only reporting API. No delete semantics apply. No tombstone or soft-delete pattern documented.
Configuration.DefaultConflictResolutionRationale: Pull-only API with no mutations. ConcurrencyControl=none means MostRecent is not applicable. DestWins is the safe default for a read-only inbound reporting feed.
Configuration.CustomObjectMarkerPatternGap: No custom object mechanism in the Reporting API GraphQL SDL. The SDL schema is static and served via SpectaQL from the live schema. No extension/custom-type pattern documented.
Configuration.CustomFieldMarkerPatternGap: The JSON scalar 'payload' on Metadata type holds open-ended JSON but is not a customer-custom field extension — it is a schema-defined field.

universalPK.note: The 'id' field appears as the primary identifier on every concrete type in the SDL that has an identity field (Assessment.id, Assignment.id, Certificate.id, Course.id, Webinar.id, Order.id, User.id, BaseTeam.id, etc.). >95% coverage across named object types. Per-IO IsPrimaryKey declarations will confirm per type.

Configuration.BatchMaxRequestCountGap: No rate limit documentation found in any credential-free source (SpectaQL docs page, Elevio help article). No X-RateLimit-* headers documented anywhere. BatchMaxRequestCount set to -1 (unknown). Implement conservative RateLimitPolicy in connector code; parse Retry-After header if returned.
Configuration.BatchRequestWaitTimeGap: Same as BatchMaxRequestCountGap — no rate window documented.
Configuration.CredentialTypeNote: Credentials are per-tenant: applicationId and applicationSecret issued by Blue Sky eLearn. Clients must contact their CSM to obtain credentials.
Configuration.SchemaURLNote: SpectaQL-rendered GraphQL schema documentation embeds the complete SDL credential-free (93 OBJECT types, 16 Query operations, 0 mutations, 0 subscriptions). Live /graphql introspection is auth-gated.
Configuration.GraphQLQueryModelNote: Two top-level scope containers: 'account' (site-wide) and 'teams' (team-scoped). All report data accessed as sub-fields with per-sub-field arguments. 16 operative top-level queries (excluding 'root' health-check).

SDLStats.note: 16 operative queries. 'root' returns String — health-check ping only. Source: SpectaQL HTML at https://data-api.pathlms.com/

fields.Name: coupons
fields.Description: This query will return the list of all the coupons in the account that have not been deleted. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

fields.Name: discounts
fields.Description: This query will return the list of all the discounts in the account that have not been deleted. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150. The difference between this query and the coupons query is the data that is returned in the fields under this top level report.

fields.Name: externalActivityReport
fields.Description: This query will return the list of all the external activities in the account that have not been deleted, falls within the date range between the specified start and end date parameters. It will also take into account a list of permitted users based off of the permissions laid out in Path. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

fields.Name: ordersReport
fields.Description: This query will return a list of Orders in the account that have not been deleted, has been paid, was created within the date range between the specified start and end date parameters, or paid at between the specified start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150. It will also take into account a list of permitted users based off of the permissions laid out in Path.

fields.Name: orderItemsReport
fields.Description: This query will return a list of Order Items in the account that have not been deleted, has been paid, was created within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

fields.Name: productCatalogReport
fields.Description: This query will return every sellable item in Path that has an item ID for an account. This item is sellable and has not been deleted. This page is commonly used to find the IDs of products for integration purposes. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

fields.Name: refundsDetailsReport
fields.Description: This query will return a list of Order Items in the account that have not been deleted, was refunded within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

fields.Name: refundsReport
fields.Description: This query will return a list of Orders in the account that have not been deleted, a Refund was created within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

fields.Name: salesByCategoryReport
fields.Description: This query will groups sales, orders and revenue by the category of items included in the purchase transaction. This uses order and order items within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

fields.Name: salesByUserReport
fields.Description: This query groups orders, revenue, and credit balances by user. This report is useful for things like: understanding all-time revenue for individual users, viewing and exporting a list of all orders for an individual user or viewing credit usage within the range of the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

fields.Name: commerceSalesByContentReport
fields.Description: This query groups sales and revenue by order item (content purchased). This report will also include archived content if the withArchived parameter is set to true. It uses the order items that were created within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

fields.Name: commerceSalesByContentDetailsReport
fields.Description: This query gets details for content purchased for an order item. It uses a course item type and id and gets orders that were created within the date range between the start and end date parameters. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.

fields.Name: userCount
fields.Description: This query will return a total count of users for an account. This count will include users that have not been deleted.

fields.Name: salesByBundleReport
fields.Description: This query retrieves a list of Order Items. It filters the results based on a set of criteria, including the date range, user IDs, and product types. It will use the offset and limit parameters to paginate the results. The default offset (page) is 0 and the default limit is 50. This limit cannot exceed 150.
